subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Addition as Grouping
description: Understanding addition as combining groups of objects

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Skip Counting
description: Counting by 2s, 5s, 10s to prepare for multiplication

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Introduction to Multiplication
description: Multiplication as repeated addition

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Multiplication as Arrays
description: Understanding rows and columns (e.g., 3×4 = 12)

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Identifying Shapes
description: Quadrilaterals, rectangles, squares

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Repeating Patterns
description: Recognizing and extending patterns

subject: PHYSICS
name: Pushes and Pulls
description: Forces cause objects to move or change

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: States of Matter
description: Solids, liquids, and gases — and how heat changes them

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Mixtures
description: Combining two or more materials that can be separated

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Solutions
description: One substance dissolves into another (like sugar in water)

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Introduction to Fractions
description: Parts of a whole — halves, thirds, quarters

subject: PHYSICS
name: Friction
description: A force that slows things down when surfaces rub together

subject: PHYSICS
name: Balance and Weight
description: Heavier objects need more force to balance; seesaws show this

subject: PHYSICS
name: Light and Shadows
description: Shadows form when objects block light; shadow length depends on the sun's angle

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Symmetry
description: A shape is symmetrical when both sides mirror each other

subject: PHYSICS
name: Density and Floating
description: Dense objects sink; less dense objects float — it's about how tightly packed the matter is

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Subtraction
description: Taking away from a group to find how many are left

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Introduction to Division
description: Division as sharing equally into groups; relationship between multiplication and division

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Place Value to Hundreds
description: Understanding the value of digits in ones, tens, and hundreds places

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Rounding to Tens and Hundreds
description: Rounding whole numbers to the nearest 10 or 100 using place value understanding

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Telling Time and Elapsed Time
description: Telling time to the nearest minute and calculating elapsed time intervals

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Introduction to Area
description: Understanding area as the number of square units covering a flat surface

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Perimeter of Shapes
description: Calculating perimeter of polygons by adding side lengths

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Fractions as Equal Parts
description: Understanding fractions as equal parts of a whole, naming unit fractions

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Reading Bar Graphs and Pictographs
description: Interpreting data displayed in bar graphs and pictographs to answer questions

subject: PHYSICS
name: Magnets and Magnetic Force
description: Understanding how magnets attract and repel, and how magnetic force acts at a distance without touching

subject: LOGIC
name: Simple Repeating Patterns
description: Identifying and extending simple AB, ABC, and ABB repeating patterns using shapes, colors, and objects

subject: LOGIC
name: Sorting and Classifying
description: Grouping objects into categories based on one attribute and explaining the sorting rule

subject: LOGIC
name: Shape Sequences
description: Identifying what comes next in a sequence of shapes that follow a rule

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Multi-Digit Multiplication by 1 Digit
description: Multiplying 2-4 digit numbers by a 1-digit number using standard algorithm

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Multi-Digit Multiplication (2-Digit)
description: Multiplying two 2-digit numbers using area model and standard algorithm

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Long Division Introduction
description: Dividing multi-digit numbers by 1-digit divisors using long division

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Multiplying and Dividing by Powers of Ten
description: Understanding how multiplying or dividing by 10, 100, 1000 shifts digits in place value

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Factors and Multiples
description: Finding all factors of numbers 1-100 and identifying multiples of given numbers

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Equivalent Fractions
description: Finding equivalent fractions using multiplication and fraction models

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Comparing Fractions
description: Comparing fractions with different numerators and denominators using benchmarks and common denominators

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Adding Fractions with Same Denominator
description: Adding and subtracting fractions and mixed numbers with the same denominator

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Decimal Notation (Tenths and Hundredths)
description: Reading, writing, and representing decimals to the hundredths place

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Unit Conversion in Measurement
description: Converting between measurement units within the same system (inches to feet, minutes to hours, etc.)

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Area of Complex Figures
description: Computing area of L-shapes and composite figures by decomposing them into rectangles

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Angles and Lines
description: Identifying and measuring angles, understanding parallel and perpendicular lines

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Introduction to Variables
description: Using letters to represent unknown quantities in simple expressions and equations

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Solving Simple Equations
description: Solving one-step equations like x + 5 = 12 or 3x = 18 using inverse operations

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Cost Formula (Price × Quantity)
description: Applying multiplication to calculate total cost in real-world shopping scenarios

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Number Patterns and Rules
description: Identifying rules in number patterns and generating the next terms in a sequence

subject: PHYSICS
name: Forms of Energy
description: Identifying different forms of energy including kinetic, potential, light, heat, and sound energy

subject: PHYSICS
name: Sound and Vibrations
description: Understanding that sound is caused by vibrations and travels through materials as waves

subject: PHYSICS
name: Light Reflection and Refraction
description: Understanding how light reflects off surfaces and bends (refracts) when passing through different materials

subject: PHYSICS
name: Energy Transfer
description: Understanding how energy moves from one object or place to another through collisions, heat, or waves

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Dissolving and Solutions
description: Understanding how solutes dissolve in solvents to form solutions, and factors affecting dissolving rate

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Properties of Materials
description: Identifying and using physical properties (hardness, flexibility, conductivity) to classify materials

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Heating and Cooling Changes
description: How heating and cooling cause changes in state (melting, freezing, evaporating, condensing)

subject: LOGIC
name: Number Sequences with Rules
description: Finding the rule in a number sequence (add 5, multiply by 2, etc.) and predicting future terms

subject: LOGIC
name: Sorting by Two Attributes
description: Classifying objects using two criteria simultaneously, introducing Venn diagram classification

subject: LOGIC
name: If-Then Reasoning
description: Understanding conditional statements: if A then B, and applying them to simple puzzles

subject: LOGIC
name: Tree Diagrams and Counting
description: Using tree diagrams to systematically count all possible outcomes in simple scenarios

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Order of Operations
description: Evaluating expressions using PEMDAS (parentheses, exponents, multiplication/division, addition/subtraction)

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Adding Fractions with Unlike Denominators
description: Finding common denominators to add and subtract fractions with different denominators

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Multiplying Fractions
description: Multiplying fractions and whole numbers, including mixed numbers

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Dividing Fractions
description: Dividing unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Decimal Operations
description: Adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing decimals to the hundredths place

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Volume of Rectangular Prisms
description: Understanding volume as filling space and calculating using l × w × h

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Coordinate Plane Basics
description: Plotting and identifying points using (x, y) coordinates in the first quadrant

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Distance-Velocity-Time Formula
description: Solving word problems using d = v × t, including finding any one of the three quantities

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Multi-Step Word Problems
description: Solving complex word problems requiring 3 or more operations and equations

subject: PHYSICS
name: Gravity
description: Understanding gravity as a force that pulls objects toward Earth and varies with mass and distance

subject: PHYSICS
name: Simple Machines
description: How levers, pulleys, ramps, and wheels and axles reduce the effort needed to do work

subject: PHYSICS
name: Conservation of Energy
description: Understanding that energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed from one form to another

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Chemical vs Physical Change
description: Distinguishing between physical changes (reversible, same substance) and chemical changes (new substance formed)

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Density
description: Understanding density as mass per volume, why objects float or sink based on density comparison

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Conservation of Mass
description: Understanding that total mass is conserved in physical and chemical changes

subject: LOGIC
name: Growing and Shrinking Patterns
description: Analyzing patterns that grow or shrink according to a rule, connecting to algebraic thinking

subject: LOGIC
name: Visual Analogies
description: Completing analogical reasoning: A is to B as C is to ? using visual patterns

subject: LOGIC
name: Basic Syllogisms
description: Reaching logical conclusions from two given statements (All A are B; All B are C; therefore...)

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Introduction to Ratios
description: Understanding and writing ratios in three forms (a:b, a/b, a to b) to compare quantities

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Unit Rates
description: Finding and using unit rates to solve real-world problems (miles per hour, price per item)

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Proportional Reasoning
description: Setting up and solving proportions to find unknown quantities in real-world situations

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Percentages
description: Converting between fractions, decimals, and percentages. Finding percent of a number.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Negative Numbers and Integers
description: Understanding positive and negative numbers on the number line, ordering and comparing integers

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Integer Operations
description: Adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing positive and negative integers

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Algebraic Expressions
description: Writing and evaluating algebraic expressions with one or two variables

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: One-Step Equations (Grade 6)
description: Solving one-step equations involving integers, fractions, and decimals

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Statistical Measures
description: Calculating and interpreting mean, median, mode, and range of data sets

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Surface Area of 3D Shapes
description: Finding surface area of rectangular prisms and triangular prisms by unfolding nets

subject: PHYSICS
name: Speed and Velocity
description: Calculating speed using distance/time, understanding direction as part of velocity

subject: PHYSICS
name: Newton's Laws of Motion
description: Understanding the three laws: inertia, F=ma, and equal-and-opposite reactions

subject: PHYSICS
name: Pressure (Force per Area)
description: Understanding pressure as force distributed over area, with real-world applications

subject: PHYSICS
name: Thermal Energy and Heat Transfer
description: Understanding temperature, thermal energy, and three types of heat transfer: conduction, convection, radiation

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Atoms and Elements
description: Understanding atoms as the building blocks of matter and elements as pure substances made of one type of atom

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Periodic Table Introduction
description: Reading the periodic table: element symbols, atomic number, groups and periods, metals vs nonmetals

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Introduction to Chemical Reactions
description: Understanding that chemical reactions rearrange atoms to form new substances with new properties

subject: LOGIC
name: Algebraic Patterns
description: Expressing pattern rules using algebraic notation and predicting terms using formulas

subject: LOGIC
name: Matrix Puzzles
description: Completing 3x3 matrices by identifying the rule that relates rows and columns

subject: LOGIC
name: Multi-Step Deductive Reasoning
description: Solving logic puzzles that require chaining several if-then statements to reach a conclusion

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Proportional Relationships and Graphs
description: Identifying proportional relationships in tables, graphs, and equations; finding the constant of proportionality

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Percent Applications
description: Solving percent increase/decrease, discount, tax, tip, and interest problems

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Rational Number Operations
description: Adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing all rational numbers including negatives, fractions, and decimals

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Linear Equations (One Variable)
description: Solving multi-step linear equations with variables on both sides including with fractions

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Introduction to Probability
description: Understanding probability as a number 0-1, calculating theoretical probability of simple events

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Scale Drawings and Similar Figures
description: Using scale factors to solve problems with scale drawings and similar geometric figures

subject: PHYSICS
name: Kinetic and Potential Energy
description: Calculating kinetic energy (KE = ½mv²) and gravitational potential energy (PE = mgh), and their interconversion

subject: PHYSICS
name: Wave Properties
description: Understanding amplitude, wavelength, frequency, and speed of waves; relationship between frequency and pitch

subject: PHYSICS
name: Electromagnetic Spectrum
description: Understanding the spectrum of electromagnetic radiation from radio waves to gamma rays

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Acids and Bases
description: Understanding the pH scale, properties of acids and bases, and neutralization reactions

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Compounds and Chemical Bonding
description: Understanding how atoms combine to form compounds, introduction to ionic and covalent bonding concepts

subject: LOGIC
name: Fibonacci-Type Sequences
description: Understanding recursive sequences where each term depends on previous terms, including Fibonacci numbers

subject: LOGIC
name: Spatial Rotation and Folding
description: Mentally rotating and folding 2D shapes to determine 3D outcomes — classic IQ test reasoning

subject: LOGIC
name: Advanced Logic Puzzles
description: Multi-variable constraint satisfaction puzzles (Zebra puzzle style) requiring systematic elimination

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Introduction to Sets
description: Understanding defined sets, equal sets, empty sets, and set membership — RSM grade 3 Q1

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Euler-Venn Diagrams
description: Using Venn diagrams to show relationships between sets, subsets, and intersections

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Set Operations: Union, Intersection, Complement
description: Computing union (A ∪ B), intersection (A ∩ B), and complement of sets

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Distance-Velocity-Time Word Problems
description: Solving word problems using d = v × t formula — RSM grade 3 Q3

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Work Formula (W = R × T)
description: Worker rate problems: if A does a job in 6 hours and B in 4, how long together?

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Multi-Step Compound Word Problems
description: Problems requiring two or more operations to solve — RSM hallmark of rigor

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Multi-Step Equations
description: Solving equations like 3(x + 2) = 21 requiring distribution and inverse operations

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Mixed Numbers and Improper Fractions
description: Converting between mixed numbers (2½) and improper fractions (5/2)

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Finding a Fraction of a Number
description: Computing (3/4) of 24, linking fractions to multiplication — RSM grade 4

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Percentages as Hundredths
description: Understanding percent as parts per hundred, converting fractions to percents

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Simultaneous Motion Problems
description: Two objects moving toward or away from each other — combined rate and gap problems

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Reading and Constructing Motion Graphs
description: Plotting and interpreting distance-time graphs, understanding slope as speed

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Area of Right Triangles
description: Computing triangle area as half the product of base and height

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Inequalities (Strict, Non-Strict, Double)
description: Understanding <, >, ≤, ≥ and solving single-variable inequalities — RSM grade 5

subject: LOGIC
name: Fundamental Counting Principle
description: If event A has m outcomes and B has n outcomes, together there are m × n outcomes

subject: LOGIC
name: Logical Connectives (If-Then, For All, There Exists)
description: Understanding conditional statements, universal and existential quantifiers

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Plotting Ordered Pairs
description: Plotting (x, y) points on a coordinate plane and reading graph coordinates

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Understanding Multiplication
description: Learn to interpret multiplication as groups of objects and understand how it helps find the total number of items.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Rounding Numbers
description: Learn to use place value understanding to round whole numbers to the nearest 10 or 100.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Understanding Fractions
description: Understand a fraction as the part of a whole when it is divided into equal parts.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Balanced and Unbalanced Forces
description: Learn how forces can make objects stay still or move by exploring balanced and unbalanced forces.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Motion and Patterns
description: Observe how objects move and look for patterns to predict how they will move next.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Electric and Magnetic Forces
description: Ask questions and explore how electric and magnetic forces can act at a distance between objects.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: What is Matter?
description: Matter is anything that takes up space and has weight. Everything around us is made of matter.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: States of Matter
description: Matter can be in different states: solid, liquid, or gas. These states describe how particles move and are arranged.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Changing Matter
description: Matter can change from one form to another, like ice melting into water or water boiling into steam, without losing its properties.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Organisms' Life Cycles
description: All living things go through stages like birth, growth, reproduction, and death, but their exact life cycles are different.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Animal Group Behaviors
description: Some animals form groups that help them find food, stay safe, or survive better together.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Traits from Parents
description: Plants and animals get traits from their parents, and these traits can be different among similar animals or plants.

subject: CODING
name: Using Variables
description: Learn how to use variables to store and change numbers in your programs.

subject: CODING
name: Sequences and Events
description: Explore how to make your programs follow steps in order and respond to clicks or key presses.

subject: CODING
name: Loops and Conditionals
description: Learn to repeat actions with loops and make decisions with if-then statements to create smarter programs.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Describe Characters
description: Students learn how to describe characters in stories, including their traits, feelings, and motivations, and understand how characters' actions move the story forward.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Sequence of Events
description: Students will practice recounting story events in the correct order and understanding how the sequence affects the story's meaning.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Story's Moral or Lesson
description: Students learn to identify the main lesson or moral in stories, especially fables, folktales, and myths, and explain how it is conveyed through story details.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: What Is Money?
description: Money is what people use to buy things and pay for services. It can be coins, bills, or digital money.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Needs and Wants
description: People need things like food and shelter, while wants are things like toys and candy. Both are part of our choices.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Saving Money
description: Saving money means setting some aside instead of spending it all now. It helps people buy bigger things later.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: What is AI?
description: Learn what Artificial Intelligence (AI) is and think about how computers can act smart.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Recognizing Patterns
description: Discover how computers find patterns like shapes, colors, or numbers to help them understand data.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Training AI
description: Learn how computers are trained using examples so they can solve problems better.

subject: LOGIC
name: Identify Patterns
description: Learn to find and complete patterns using colors, shapes, or numbers.

subject: LOGIC
name: Sort by Category
description: Practice grouping objects or ideas into different categories based on similarities.

subject: LOGIC
name: Find the Next in Sequence
description: Learn to predict the next item in a sequence of shapes, numbers, or patterns.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Understanding Place Value
description: Explore how digits in a number represent different amounts depending on their position, including units, tens, hundreds, and beyond.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Dividing Whole Numbers
description: Learn how to interpret and perform division of whole numbers, including sharing objects equally and understanding quotients.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Understanding Fractions
description: Recognize a fraction as a part of a whole, understanding numerator and denominator and how they represent equal parts.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Balanced and Unbalanced Forces
description: Learn how forces can push or pull objects and how balanced forces keep objects still or moving at constant speed, while unbalanced forces change their motion.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Predicting Motion
description: Observe how objects move and use patterns to guess what will happen next in their motion.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Force and Energy Relationship
description: Explore how the strength of an object’s movement relates to the energy it has and transfers.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Wave Patterns and Movement
description: Understand how waves have patterns like amplitude and wavelength, and how they can make objects move.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: What is an Atom?
description: An atom is the smallest unit of matter that makes up everything around us.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Forming Molecules
description: Molecules are formed when two or more atoms join together, making new substances.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: What Are Chemical Reactions?
description: Chemical reactions happen when substances break apart or combine to make new substances.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Traits from Parents
description: Animals and plants inherit traits from their parents, which can make them look or behave a certain way.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Traits and Environment
description: Traits of plants and animals can be affected by their environment, changing how they grow or look.

subject: CODING
name: Understanding Variables
description: Learn what variables are and how they can store different kinds of information in a program.

subject: CODING
name: Creating Sequences
description: Explore how to write simple instructions in order to make a program perform a sequence of actions.

subject: CODING
name: Using Loops
description: Discover how loops can help repeat actions in a program to save time and effort.

subject: CODING
name: Conditional Statements
description: Learn how to use if-else statements to make decisions in a program based on different conditions.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Identifying Characters
description: Learn to recognize and describe characters in a story, including their traits and feelings.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Story Summaries
description: Practice recounting stories, including key events and lessons from fables and folktales.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Finding the Theme
description: Learn to determine the central message or moral of a story using details from the text.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Analyzing Characters
description: Describe how characters' traits and actions help move the story forward and lead to outcomes.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Supply and Demand
description: Supply and demand are how businesses decide how much of a product to make and how much people want to buy it.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Money and Currency
description: Money is what we use to buy things, and currency is the actual coins or bills used as money.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Producers and Consumers
description: Producers make goods and services, while consumers buy and use them.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Saving and Spending
description: Saving is keeping money for later, while spending is using money now to buy things.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: What is AI?
description: Learn what artificial intelligence (AI) means and how computers can think and learn like humans.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Machine Learning Basics
description: Discover how computers can learn from data to improve their tasks without being reprogrammed each time.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Training Data
description: Understand how examples and data help AI systems learn and make decisions.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: AI in Our Lives
description: Learn how AI is used in everyday life, like in video games, smart devices, and recommendations.

subject: LOGIC
name: Simple Patterns
description: Learn to recognize and finish basic patterns like colors, shapes, or numbers.

subject: LOGIC
name: Following Sequences
description: Understand how to follow and predict the next item in a sequence of pictures or numbers.

subject: LOGIC
name: Making Choices
description: Practice making decisions based on clues or rules, like choosing the best option among several.

subject: LOGIC
name: Sorting Items
description: Learn to group objects based on shared features, like color, shape, or size.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Place Value in Multi-Digit Numbers
description: Understanding how each digit in a multi-digit number represents a value based on its position, including the concept that each place is ten times the value to its right.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Interpreting Multiplication as Comparison
description: Learn to interpret a multiplication equation as a comparison, such as understanding that 35 = 5 × 7 means 35 is 5 times as many as 7.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Finding Factor Pairs
description: Discover all factor pairs of whole numbers from 1 to 100 and understand their relationship to multiples.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Adding Decimals to Hundredths
description: Learn to add decimals up to hundredths using models, drawings, and strategies based on place value.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Area and Perimeter of Rectangles
description: Apply formulas for area and perimeter to solve real-world and mathematical problems involving rectangles.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Energy and Speed
description: This concept explains how the speed of an object is related to its energy, showing that faster objects have more energy.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Energy Transfer Methods
description: This concept describes how energy can be transferred between objects through light, heat, sound, and electric currents.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Wave Patterns and Properties
description: This concept introduces waves, including how amplitude and wavelength create wave patterns and how waves can move objects.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Light and Reflection
description: This concept explains how light reflects from objects and enters our eyes, helping us see objects around us.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Particles of Matter
description: This concept describes how matter is made up of tiny particles that are too small to see.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Particles of Matter
description: Matter is made up of tiny particles that are too small to see without a microscope.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Energy Transfer
description: Energy can move from one place to another through light, sound, heat, or electric currents.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Conservation of Weight
description: When substances are heated, cooled, or mixed, their total weight stays the same, showing matter is conserved.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Plant Internal Structures
description: Plants have different parts inside that help them grow and stay healthy, like roots, stems, and leaves.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Animal External Structures
description: Animals have outside body parts like fur, feathers, or shells that help them survive and protect themselves.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: How Animals Use Senses
description: Animals use their senses, like sight and smell, to receive information, process it in their brains, and respond in different ways.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Materials for Plant Growth
description: Plants mainly get materials needed for growth from air (carbon dioxide) and water, which help them make food through photosynthesis.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Movement of Matter
description: Matter such as water, air, and nutrients move between plants, animals, decomposers, and the environment in a cycle.

subject: CODING
name: Introduction to Coding
description: Learn what coding is and how it is used to create computer programs and games.

subject: CODING
name: Understanding Sequences
description: Discover how sequences of commands are used to tell a computer what to do step by step.

subject: CODING
name: Using Loops
description: Learn how to use loops to repeat actions, making programs more efficient and easier to write.

subject: CODING
name: Debugging Programs
description: Practice finding and fixing errors in programs to ensure they work correctly.

subject: CODING
name: Respect for Creativity
description: Learn why it is important to give credit when using or remixing others' code.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Finding Plot Details
description: Identify important events in a story by paying attention to details that move the story forward.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Determining Story Themes
description: Discover the main message or lesson in a story by analyzing details and characters’ responses.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Using Text Evidence
description: Practice quoting and citing parts of a story to support your understanding of what it means.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Writing Character Descriptions
description: Learn to write detailed descriptions of characters using specific details from stories.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Goods and Services
description: Goods are physical items like toys or food, while services are work done for you like haircuts or car repairs.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Price and Value
description: Price is how much money something costs, and value is how much you think it’s worth to you.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Machine Learning Basics
description: Discover how computers can learn from data to make decisions without being directly programmed.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Training Data
description: Understand what training data is and how it helps AI systems learn and improve.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Neural Networks
description: Explore how neural networks mimic the brain to help AI recognize patterns and make decisions.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: AI in Real Life
description: See how AI is used in voice assistants, games, and other technology that we use daily.

subject: LOGIC
name: Continuing Patterns
description: Practice predicting what comes next in a pattern based on the sequence of shapes, colors, or numbers.

subject: LOGIC
name: Classify Objects
description: Learn how to group objects based on common features like size, color, or shape to understand similarities and differences.

subject: LOGIC
name: Decide True or False
description: Evaluate simple statements to decide whether they are true or false based on given information.

subject: LOGIC
name: Draw Basic Conclusions
description: Use clues and evidence to make simple logical conclusions about a situation or story.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Adding Fractions with Different Denominators
description: Learn to find equivalent fractions and add fractions with unlike denominators by creating common denominators.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Using a Coordinate System
description: Use a pair of perpendicular number lines, called axes, to locate points and understand positions on a grid.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Understanding Ratios
description: Understand and describe relationships between two quantities using ratio language and notation.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Dividing Fractions
description: Learn how to interpret and compute the division of fractions, solving real-world problems involving fractional division.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Volume of Rectangular Prisms
description: Understand the concept of volume as the space inside a solid figure and learn how to measure it.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Conservation of Weight
description: When substances are heated or mixed, their total weight stays the same, showing that mass is conserved.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Material Properties
description: Different materials have unique properties that help us identify them, like color, texture, and hardness.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Gravity and Downward Force
description: Earth pulls objects down due to gravity, which is a force that acts toward the center of the planet.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Energy from the Sun
description: Animals get energy from food that originally comes from the sun, linking sunlight to energy used by living things.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Particle Motion and State Changes
description: Adding or removing thermal energy causes particles to move faster or slower, changing the state of the material (solid, liquid, gas).

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Particles Too Small to See
description: Matter is made of tiny particles that are so small we cannot see them without special tools, but they make up everything around us.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Matter is Not Created or Destroyed
description: When substances are heated, cooled, or mixed, the total weight stays the same, showing matter is conserved during changes.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Detecting Chemical Reactions
description: Observing changes in properties or energy can help us tell if a chemical reaction has taken place between materials.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Energy from the Sun in Food
description: Animals get energy from their food, which originally comes from the sun, to help them grow, move, and stay warm.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Atoms in Molecules
description: Atoms are the small building blocks that connect to form molecules and extended structures like crystals and plastics.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Plant Materials from Air and Water
description: Plants mainly get the materials they need for growth from air and water, which help them produce food through photosynthesis.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Model of Matter Movement
description: A model showing how matter moves among plants, animals, decomposers, and the environment in an ecosystem.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Cells are Made of Parts
description: Living things are made of cells, and investigating reveals that cells are the basic units of life, whether one or many.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Cell Parts and Functions
description: Different parts of a cell work together to help the cell carry out its specific functions in the organism.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Body as a System
description: The human body is made up of interacting subsystems, such as the circulatory, respiratory, and digestive systems, that work together.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Cell Division and Growth
description: Cell division helps organisms grow, heal, and replace worn-out cells, which is important for development and repair.

subject: CODING
name: Control Structures
description: Understand how to use if-else statements and loops to control the flow of a program.

subject: CODING
name: Flowcharts and Pseudocode
description: Use flowcharts and pseudocode to plan and visualize solving a coding problem step-by-step.

subject: CODING
name: Data Encoding Methods
description: Explore different ways to represent data using encoding schemes like binary and ASCII.

subject: CODING
name: Data Packets and Networks
description: Learn how information is broken into packets, transmitted over networks, and reassembled at the destination.

subject: CODING
name: Debugging and Troubleshooting
description: Discover techniques for finding and fixing errors in your code to improve program reliability.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Identifying Character Traits
description: Students learn to recognize and describe the personality traits of characters in stories through actions and dialogue.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Summarizing Plot
description: Students develop skills to create concise summaries of story events, recognizing beginning, middle, and end elements.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Determining Theme
description: Students analyze details in a story to identify the main message or lesson the author conveys.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Supporting with Evidence
description: Students practice quoting and citing specific parts of the text to support their explanations and inferences.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Analyzing Figurative Language
description: Students interpret figurative language such as metaphors and similes to understand tone and meaning.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Writing Narratives
description: Students learn to write stories with clear sequence, descriptive details, and engaging events based on real or imagined experiences.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Goods and Services
description: Goods are physical items like toys, and services are actions like haircuts that people pay for.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Wants and Needs
description: Wants are things we desire, while needs are essentials like food and water that we must have.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Saving and Spending
description: Saving means setting aside money for later, while spending means using money to buy things now.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: How Goods Are Made
description: Production is the process of creating goods and services that people want to buy.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Trade and Resources
description: Trade allows countries to buy and sell goods and resources they might not have locally.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: What is AI?
description: Learn what artificial intelligence (AI) is and how computers can think and learn.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Introduction to Machine Learning
description: Discover how computers can learn from data to make decisions without being explicitly programmed.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Gathering Data
description: Learn how collecting good data is important for training AI and machines to learn effectively.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Training AI Models
description: Understand how AI systems are trained using data to recognize patterns and make predictions.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: AI Ethics & Safety
description: Explore how to create AI responsibly and safely, understanding the importance of ethics in AI.

subject: LOGIC
name: Recognizing Patterns
description: Learn how to identify repeating shapes, colors, or numbers in sequences.

subject: LOGIC
name: True or False Statements
description: Practice deciding if statements are true or false based on information given.

subject: LOGIC
name: Classifying Items
description: Learn how to group objects or ideas into categories based on shared features.

subject: LOGIC
name: Simple Deductive Reasoning
description: Use clues and facts to figure out what is true or what will happen next.

subject: LOGIC
name: Using Venn Diagrams
description: Learn to compare groups and see how they overlap using Venn diagrams.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Understanding Ratios
description: Learn what ratios are and how to describe the relationship between two quantities using ratio language.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Defining Unit Rates
description: Understand what a unit rate is and how to compute it from ratios in real-world contexts.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Dividing Fractions
description: Learn how to interpret and compute quotients of fractions and solve related word problems.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Adding Rational Numbers
description: Add and subtract rational numbers, including positive and negative numbers, in various contexts.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Solving Linear Equations
description: Use algebraic methods to solve simple linear equations and inequalities with variables.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Scale Drawings
description: Solve problems involving scale drawings to find actual lengths and areas of geometric figures.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Properties of Matter
description: Learn about different properties of materials, such as size, shape, and state, to understand how substances behave.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Atoms and Molecules
description: Discover how atoms are the building blocks of matter and how they combine to form molecules.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Thermal Energy and Temperature
description: Understand how adding or removing heat affects the motion of particles and changes the temperature of a substance.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Newton's Third Law
description: Learn that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction and how this affects moving objects.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Waves and Properties
description: Explore how waves carry energy, and learn about amplitude, wavelength, and how waves behave when they encounter different materials.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Simple Molecular Structures
description: Models show how atoms combine to form simple molecules, helping us understand the basic structure of common substances.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Signs of Chemical Reactions
description: When substances react, they often change in appearance or produce something new. Recognizing these signs helps us tell if a chemical change has happened.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Natural and Synthetic Materials
description: Materials come from natural resources or are created in labs. Understanding their origins shows their impact on society.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Particle Motion and Temperature
description: Adding or removing heat causes particles to move faster or slower, leading to changes in the state of a substance like melting or boiling.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Conservation of Mass
description: In a chemical reaction, the total number of atoms remains the same, meaning mass is conserved even as substances change.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Chemical Bonding
description: Atoms bond together through different types of bonds, which determine the structure and properties of molecules.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Cells as Building Blocks
description: Living things are made of cells; some are single-celled while others have many cells working together.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Cell Division and Growth
description: Cells divide to help organisms grow, repair, and replace damaged cells.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Photosynthesis and Energy
description: Plants use photosynthesis to turn sunlight into energy, which is essential for their growth and for food in ecosystems.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Chemical Reactions in Food
description: Organisms break down food molecules through chemical reactions to release energy and build new molecules.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Ecosystem Energy Flow
description: Energy moves through ecosystems from producers to consumers, and matter is cycled between living and nonliving parts.

subject: CODING
name: Understanding Algorithms
description: Learn what algorithms are and how they help us solve problems step-by-step using flowcharts or pseudocode.

subject: CODING
name: Variables and Data Types
description: Discover how to create variables to store different types of data like numbers and text, and understand how to use them in programs.

subject: CODING
name: Control Structures
description: Learn to use conditionals and loops to control the flow of a program, including nested loops for complex decision making.

subject: CODING
name: Decomposing Problems
description: Practice breaking down complex problems into smaller parts to make coding easier and more manageable.

subject: CODING
name: Creating Procedures
description: Learn to write procedures or functions with parameters to organize code, improve reusability, and simplify complex tasks.

subject: CODING
name: Testing and Debugging
description: Explore methods to test your programs with different data and find or fix errors to improve reliability.

subject: CODING
name: Data Representation
description: Understand how data can be represented using different encoding schemes like binary, and how computers process this data.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Literary Devices
description: An introduction to common literary devices like metaphor, simile, and personification that help create meaning in texts.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Character Analysis
description: Learning how to examine characters' traits, motivations, and development throughout a story.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Identifying Themes
description: Discovering the central ideas or messages in a story and how authors convey them through details.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Plot Structure
description: Understanding the components of a plot, such as beginning, middle, climax, and resolution, and how they shape a story.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Point of View
description: Exploring different narrative perspectives like first person, third person, and how they influence a story's presentation.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Poetry Analysis
description: Learning how to analyze poetry for structure, figurative language, tone, and meaning.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Drama Elements
description: Understanding elements of drama such as dialogue, acts, scenes, and stage directions to interpret plays.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Supply and Demand
description: Supply and demand are the key forces that affect prices and availability of goods and services in a market.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Scarcity and Value
description: Scarcity means resources are limited, which affects how we decide to use them to meet our needs and wants.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Money as Exchange
description: Money is used as a tool for exchanging goods and services, making trade easier than bartering.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Production and Consumption
description: Production involves making goods or services, while consumption is when people use or buy those goods and services.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Saving and Investing
description: Saving involves setting aside money for the future, while investing aims to grow money through different financial tools.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Benefits of Trade
description: Trade allows countries and people to access goods and services they might not produce themselves, leading to more choices and better prices.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Market Economy
description: A market economy is a system where prices and production are determined by buyers and sellers in the marketplace.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Machine Learning Basics
description: Discover how computers can learn from data to make decisions without being explicitly programmed.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Neural Networks Intro
description: Explore how neural networks mimic the human brain to process information and recognize patterns.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: AI Ethics
description: Understand the ethical questions and responsibilities involved in developing and using AI.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: The Future of AI
description: Explore potential future developments and challenges in artificial intelligence technology.

subject: LOGIC
name: Understanding Logical Statements
description: Learn what logical statements are and how they form the building blocks of reasoning.

subject: LOGIC
name: Truth and Falsehood
description: Discover how statements can be true or false, and why this is important in logic.

subject: LOGIC
name: Logical Operators: AND, OR, NOT
description: Explore how combining statements with AND, OR, and NOT changes their meaning and truth.

subject: LOGIC
name: Using Truth Tables
description: Learn how to create truth tables to analyze the truth of complex logical statements.

subject: LOGIC
name: Conditional Statements
description: Understand if-then statements and how they relate to logical reasoning.

subject: LOGIC
name: Biconditional Logic
description: Learn about statements that are true when both parts are equally true or false.

subject: LOGIC
name: Valid Deductions
description: Discover how to make valid conclusions based on given logical premises and rules.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Calculating Unit Rates
description: Compute the unit rate from a ratio of fractions, applying this skill to real-world problems involving different units.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Multiplying Rational Numbers
description: Use previous understanding of fractions to multiply and divide rational numbers, including negatives.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Graphing Proportional Relationships
description: Interpret and graph proportional relationships, understanding that the slope represents the unit rate.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Understanding Irrational Numbers
description: Recognize that some numbers are not rational and understand their properties, including their decimal expansion.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Speed and Velocity
description: Understanding the difference between speed and velocity helps explain how objects move in a straight line and in different directions.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Kinetic Energy
description: Kinetic energy is the energy an object has because of its motion, which depends on its mass and speed.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Potential Energy
description: Potential energy is stored energy in an object due to its position or configuration, like a stretched spring or elevated object.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Energy Transfer
description: Energy can be transferred between objects or systems when their kinetic or potential energy changes, often involving work and force.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Wave Amplitude and Energy
description: In waves, the amplitude is related to the amount of energy the wave carries; larger amplitudes mean more energy.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Wave Behavior in Materials
description: Waves can be reflected, absorbed, or transmitted when they encounter different materials, affecting how energy moves through media.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Forces and Motion
description: Forces cause changes in motion, including starting, stopping, or changing the direction of an object.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Newton's Laws
description: Newton's Laws describe how forces affect the motion of objects, including concepts like inertia and acceleration.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Properties of Matter
description: Learn about the different properties that describe and identify matter, such as density, color, and state.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Atoms as Building Blocks
description: Understand that atoms are the smallest units of matter that retain the properties of an element.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Periodic Table Overview
description: Learn how elements are organized in the periodic table and what information it provides about each element.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Chemical Bonding
description: Explore how atoms connect to form molecules through different types of chemical bonds.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Acids and Bases
description: Explore the properties of acids and bases, and how they react with each other in chemical reactions.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Photosynthesis and Ecosystems
description: Learn how photosynthesis helps plants produce energy and supports the flow of energy in ecosystems.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Chemical Reactions in Cells
description: Explore how food molecules are broken down and built up through chemical reactions to support growth and energy.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Effects of Resources on Organisms
description: Understand how the availability of resources like food and water influences the health and size of populations.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Food and Matter Cycles
description: Develop models showing how food energy and matter move and change through organisms and their environment.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Genetic Mutations and Proteins
description: Learn how tiny changes in genes can affect the proteins in an organism and its traits.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Asexual and Sexual Reproduction
description: Compare how offspring are produced with identical or geneticly varied traits through different reproduction methods.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Fossil Record and Evolution
description: Analyze patterns in fossils to understand how life has changed, diversified, and gone extinct over Earth's history.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Genetic Variation and Survival
description: Explain how differences in traits within a population can help some individuals survive and reproduce better in their environment.

subject: CODING
name: Functions with Parameters
description: Create reusable blocks of code called functions that can take parameters to perform specific tasks multiple times.

subject: CODING
name: Using Libraries and Media
description: Understand how to include external libraries, media, and pre-made code to enhance your programs and give proper attribution.

subject: CODING
name: Code Comments and Documentation
description: Learn how to add comments to your code to explain what it does, making it easier to follow, test, and debug.

subject: CODING
name: Collaborative Design
description: Explore ways to work with others to gather feedback, refine your programs, and create solutions that meet user needs.

subject: CODING
name: Basic Encryption
description: Understand simple methods of encoding information to keep digital data secure during transmission.

subject: CODING
name: Tech Ethics and Bias
description: Discuss issues of bias, accessibility, and privacy in technology design to create fair and inclusive digital tools.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Character Development
description: Examining how characters change and grow throughout a story and how they influence the plot.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Theme Development
description: Understanding how central themes emerge and change over the course of a text and their impact on the overall message.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Symbolism and Motifs
description: Exploring how symbols and recurring motifs contribute to the meaning and depth of a literary work.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Text Structure Analysis
description: Analyzing how the structure of a poem or drama (e.g., sonnet, soliloquy) helps convey its meaning.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Author's Perspective
description: Determining an author's point of view or purpose and how they distinguish their position from others.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Dramatic Irony and Effects
description: Analyzing how different points of view and dramatic irony create suspense, humor, or other effects in a story.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Understanding Scarcity
description: Scarcity means there are limited resources to meet unlimited wants and needs, forcing people to make choices.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Opportunity Cost
description: Opportunity cost is what you give up when you make a choice, highlighting the benefits of the next best alternative.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: What is Money?
description: Money is a medium of exchange used to buy goods and services instead of bartering.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Banking and Savings
description: Banking involves storing money, earning interest, and loans, which help people and businesses grow financially.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Entrepreneurship
description: Entrepreneurship is the process of starting and running a new business to offer goods or services.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Economies of Scale
description: Economies of scale occur when the cost per unit of producing goods decreases as production increases.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Interest Rates
description: Interest rates are the cost of borrowing money, influencing saving and spending habits.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: What Is Artificial Intelligence?
description: Learn about what AI is and how it is used in everyday life.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Training Data and Models
description: Learn about training data and how machines create models to interpret data patterns.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Natural Language Processing
description: Discover how AI understands and works with human language and communication.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Computer Vision Basics
description: Learn how AI systems interpret and analyze images and videos.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Ethics in AI
description: Understand the moral and ethical issues surrounding AI development and use.

subject: LOGIC
name: Understanding Propositions
description: Learn what propositions are and how they form the building blocks of logical statements.

subject: LOGIC
name: Logical Connectives
description: Discover common connectives like AND, OR, and NOT that connect propositions to form complex statements.

subject: LOGIC
name: Logical Inference
description: Understand how to draw logical conclusions from true premises using inference rules.

subject: LOGIC
name: Biconditional Logic
description: Explore statements that are true when two propositions are both true or both false.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Rational and Irrational Numbers
description: Students understand the difference between rational and irrational numbers and learn how to identify and convert decimals that repeat into rational numbers.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Estimating Irrational Numbers
description: Students use rational approximations to compare and locate irrational numbers such as π on a number line and estimate their values.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Properties of Exponents
description: Students apply the laws of exponents to simplify and generate equivalent numerical expressions involving integer exponents.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Square and Cube Roots
description: Students use square root and cube root symbols to solve equations of the form x² = p and x³ = p and evaluate of roots of perfect squares and cubes.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Scientific Notation
description: Students learn to perform operations with numbers in scientific notation, estimating very large or small quantities and interpreting notation generated by technology.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Slope and Line Equations
description: Students understand that the slope m is the same between any two points on a line and derive equations y=mx and y=mx+b.

subject: MATHEMATICS
name: Functions and Graphs
description: Students understand that a function assigns exactly one output to each input, interpret various representations of functions, and explore their properties.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Newton's Laws of Motion
description: Fundamental principles explaining how objects move and respond to forces, including inertia and acceleration.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Force and Acceleration
description: Explores how the application of force causes objects to accelerate according to Newton's second law.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Energy and Work
description: Introduces different forms of energy and how work is done when energy is transferred by forces.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Kinetic and Potential Energy
description: Explains types of mechanical energy, how they transform into each other, and their role in motion.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Waves and Sound
description: Examines how waves travel through different media, including the properties of sound waves and their detection.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Light and Reflection
description: Describes how light behaves, including reflection, refraction, and the formation of images.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Electric Current and Circuits
description: Introduces electric charge, current flow, and basic circuit components like batteries and bulbs.

subject: PHYSICS
name: Magnetism and Fields
description: Explores how magnets attract or repel and how magnetic fields are created around magnetic objects.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: What is Matter?
description: An introduction to matter, including its states and properties that make up everything around us.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Atom Basics
description: Understanding atoms as the fundamental building blocks of matter, including protons, neutrons, and electrons.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Periodic Trends
description: Examining patterns such as atomic size, ionization energy, and electronegativity across periods and groups.

subject: CHEMISTRY
name: Chemical Energy
description: Understanding how chemical reactions involve energy changes, including exothermic and endothermic processes.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Cell Structure
description: Learn about the parts of a cell and their functions to understand how cells carry out life processes.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: DNA and Proteins
description: Discover how DNA stores genetic information and how it directs the production of proteins in cells.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Mutations and Effects
description: Explore how changes to genes (mutations) can affect organism traits and health, sometimes causing harmful or beneficial effects.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Reproduction Types
description: Understand the differences between asexual and sexual reproduction and how they influence genetic diversity.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Fossil Record
description: Analyze fossil evidence to learn about the history of life, including species extinction and evolutionary changes.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Evolutionary Relationships
description: Compare and interpret similarities and differences among organisms to understand how they are related through evolution.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Genetic Variation
description: Study how genetic differences within a population increase survival chances and influence evolution.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: Biotechnology and Inheritance
description: Learn about technological advances that enable humans to influence inheritance and traits in organisms.

subject: BIOLOGY
name: DNA and Protein Relationship
description: Explain how the structure of DNA determines the structure and function of proteins essential for life.

subject: CODING
name: Lists and Arrays
description: Use lists and arrays to organize and manage multiple data items efficiently.

subject: CODING
name: Functions and Procedures
description: Create reusable blocks of code with functions to solve problems systematically.

subject: CODING
name: Cryptography Basics
description: Explore methods of encryption to understand how information can be kept secure during transmission.

subject: CODING
name: Public and Private Information
description: Learn the tradeoffs between sharing information publicly and keeping it private and secure.

subject: CODING
name: Collaborative Coding
description: Use strategies like surveys and crowdsourcing to work effectively with many contributors on a project.

subject: CODING
name: Algorithm Design
description: Develop step-by-step procedures to solve specific problems efficiently using algorithms.

subject: CODING
name: Ethical Impacts of Computing
description: Evaluate how computing technologies affect society, including ethical, social, and economic considerations.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Theme Development
description: Analyzing how themes or central ideas are developed and refined throughout a literary work, including character and plot influences.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Character Perspectives
description: Examining how different points of view of characters influence the story and create effects like suspense or humor.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Citing Textual Evidence
description: Learning to cite specific evidence from a text to support analysis, inference, or argument.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Literary Analysis Skills
description: Developing skills to analyze literary texts deeply, including identifying themes, literary devices, and authors’ intent.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Author’s Point of View
description: Determining an author's perspective or purpose in a literary work and understanding how rhetoric advances this purpose.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Literary Techniques
description: Identifying and analyzing techniques such as irony, symbolism, and metaphor used to enhance meaning.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Global Cultural Perspectives
description: Analyzing how different cultural experiences and points of view are reflected in literature from around the world.

subject: ENGLISH_LITERATURE
name: Writing Arguments
description: Constructing clear and convincing arguments supported by relevant evidence and reasoning.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Understanding Scarcity
description: Scarcity means resources are limited compared to needs and wants, which is the foundation of economic decision-making.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Price Fluctuations
description: Prices change based on shifts in supply and demand, affecting consumers and producers.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Consumer Choice
description: Consumers decide what to buy based on preferences, prices, and income levels.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Factors of Production
description: Resources like land, labor, capital, and entrepreneurship are needed to produce goods and services.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Economic Growth
description: Economic growth is an increase in a country’s production of goods and services over time.

subject: ECONOMICS
name: Inflation and Deflation
description: Inflation is rising prices; deflation is falling prices, both impacting purchasing power.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: What is Artificial Intelligence?
description: An overview of what artificial intelligence is and how it affects our daily lives.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Training Data in AI
description: Learning about how data is used to train AI models to improve their performance.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: AI in Real World
description: Discovering how AI is used in fields like healthcare, gaming, and robotics.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Bias in Artificial Intelligence
description: Understanding how bias can enter AI systems and ways to minimize it.

subject: AI_LEARNING
name: Creating Basic AI Projects
description: Guided activities to help students build simple AI models using accessible tools.

subject: LOGIC
name: Logic Statements
description: Understanding basic statements that can be true or false, which are the foundation of logical reasoning.

subject: LOGIC
name: Logical AND (Conjunction)
description: A logical operation that results in true only if both statements involved are true.

subject: LOGIC
name: Logical OR (Disjunction)
description: A logical operation that results in true if at least one of the statements is true.

subject: LOGIC
name: Logical NOT (Negation)
description: A logical operation that inverts the truth value of a statement, making true into false and vice versa.

subject: LOGIC
name: Truth Tables
description: A chart used to determine the truth value of complex logical expressions based on their components.

subject: LOGIC
name: Logical Equivalence
description: When two statements always have the same truth value in all scenarios, meaning they are logically equivalent.

subject: LOGIC
name: Deductive Reasoning
description: A logical process where conclusions are drawn definitely based on general principles or premises.

subject: LOGIC
name: Inductive Reasoning
description: A reasoning process that involves making generalizations based on specific observations or examples.

subject: LOGIC
name: Logical Judgment
description: The ability to evaluate arguments and determine whether they are valid or invalid.
